original text:
This Hell! government against itself: The state radical democracy Venezuela.
government against itself: The state radical democracy Venezuela.
Chavez gives this speech late 2012 where he's berating his government ministers it's his last major speech and part what he's trying lay out his legacy, and make clear what stands for. And that speech becomes perfectly clear stands for the commune, and the development alternative form production and radical democracy that stands against the existing state.
Political theorist George explores the political and economic history the Venezuelan commune, from its pre-Chavez roots popular uprising against the neoliberal order, the challenges sustaining direct democracy and socialist principles the face vast demographic changes, oil market fluctuations and capitalist resistance.
George author and Associate Professor Politics and Global Studies Drexel University Philadelphia.

original text:
Join from Fire Earth, 872 Mill Hollow Rd., Eureka Springs, Arkansas, for one-day work: Backyard Medicinals: Into the Wild with Steven Foster. This the only Arkansas event have scheduled for 2014!
For details Fire Earth's workshop page.
Early bird registration deadline extended until April 15th. $75.00. After April 15th, $95.00.
Also checkout Melissa Clare's Plant Spirit Communications event, and Lorna Trigg's Creating Medicine Wheel Garden Fire Earth, April 27th. Make weekend it.
The Ozarks have nearly 3,000 species plants, which includes about 25% that are edible and medicinal, perhaps 500 species. helpful understand that different plants occur different habitats. will look which plants you might find specific habitat. Spring our best season for wild edibles and medicinal plants, the time when get dandelion greens, wild onions, pepper grass, plantain, pokeweed, and purslane, among others and good time year enjoy edible flowers such violets and elderflowers. Come prepared learn, enjoy, and ask questions.
Japanese honeysuckle, some weed, medicinal plant.
segments the conservation community the question has arisen whether the possibility exists that some plants considered weeds the United States may have some kind economic value terms medicinal food use that could translate into commercial harvest, and hence opportunity control the weeds.
Japanese honeysuckle produces two major ingredients Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM). The flowers are known jin yin hua which consists the dried, unopened buds Japanese honeysuckle. Another product the dried stems with leaves attached known ren dong teng. The Chinese name jin yin hua means gold and silver flower. This refers the fact that the white flowers turn golden yellow one two days after blooming. The Chinese name, referring the stems with the leaves attached, ren dong, means "stand winter" reference the fact that the leaves are evergreen.
more info.......well come the workshop!!!!

From the DJFL HOME Page, click Edit Account the bottom the left hand menu. Follow the instructions the next page and then click your name edit your information. Make sure the check box "no emails" NOT checked and make sure all the email addresses you want receive notices are there. Put semicolon and space between email addresses. sure click the Submit button when you are done.
Ideally, you want add BOTH parents when setting the player account. add guardian later, scroll the bottom player record (see above for the Edit Account instructions) and click the box "clone adult" and then enter the name the guardian, cell phone and email address (and any other information that different from the player. You can delete the DOB and Grade).
sure click the Submit button when you are done.
4-Be sure that the "NO EMAILS" box NOT checked off.

You can delete the DOB and Grade).

4-Be sure that the "NO EMAILS" box NOT checked off.

original text:
Edison World School Abu Dhabi information page.
Received offer teach ESL never applied.
Offer was valid after paid $2500USD for Visa fee, goat fee, fee fee, tax fee..Etc.
What load Horse Dump!
Meinkrys wrote: Offer was valid after paid $2500USD for Visa fee, goat fee, fee fee, tax fee..Etc.
Thanks for posting here and alerting other readers the teaching job scam. really stings lose that money. don't know any easy ways get that money back. There have been many similar stories about other job scams the UAE and I've not heard any where someone got their money back.
TL;DR version information below: The Edison World School Abu Dhabi currently found online does not exist real life, and example common scam where someone advertises teaching jobs schools the UAE which don't exist. The scam that when offer made, money requested pay for visa processing fees. Never send money for visa fees any employer agency the UAE. employer agency asks for money, assume scam, the employer unpleasant work for they really exist.
Edison World School was officially announced 2011 school planned for Abu Dhabi but nothing further developed. appears that someone someones have either read that announcement and used that name for scam, set scam which coincidentally has the same name the proposed school. seems unlikely that the organisation which made the original announcement involved the teaching job scam.
what appears the official school website, however copied from the Repton School Abu Dhabi website (Repton real school), with Repton School replaced Edison School Edison World School, and some other adjustments for address, location, staff names etc. The fake EWS website even has photos Repton School Derbyshire, relabeled photos EWS.
Repton Abu Dhabi website wrote: For those families relocating back the UK, considering Boarding for their child, both Repton and Foremarke Hall (Repton's preparatory school) offers continuity curriculum and culture.
EWS website wrote: For those families relocating back the UK, considering Boarding for their child, both Edison World School and Foremarke Hall (Edison World School's preparatory school) offers continuity curriculum and culture.
The headteacher EWS according their website Bianca Anna Gabriel (or Ana). Whoever she (independent confirmation not found), the photo the website different person, Tammie McKenzie, who principal (previously assistant principal) Carroll High School, part the Carroll Community School District the city Carroll Iowa, USA. She still there far can tell (according her website, her LinkedIn page, and the school website), and probably doesn't know about her doppelganger Abu Dhabi.
There are three (or more) Edison World School domains.
currently used for the school website. Whois data shows was first registered Sep 2016.
was previously used for the school website but now just spam, possibly still used for emails. Don't visit, loads another noisy spam website. Whois data shows was first registered Aug 2016.
Repton School Abu Dhabi website has popup warning (they don't name the fake school though).
Repton Abu Dhabi website wrote: has come our attention there fake organisation sending offer letters prospective employees with the Repton Abu Dhabi branding. would like inform you that Repton Abu Dhabi way affiliated with this organisation.
Repton Abu Dhabi would never ask candidate for fee for position the school. Please vigilant when receiving offer letters from third party organisations.
(website content longer available directly) was used for EWS but website was copied from the Mayoor Indian School Abu Dhabi real school), with Find and Replace done the Mayoor school name. Whois data shows was first registered May 2016 which seems anomaly given that Google has record the website saved before that date, for example Nov 2015 for the page Possible explanation that Google took the date from the page rather than actually retrieving the page that date, since the page (which EWS copied) also has the date Nov 2015 recorded Google.
Principal Headteacher's name given website Yogita Anna.
Welcome Mayoor Private School LLC Abu Dhabi. This school has been set collaboration with the 138 year old Mayo College General Council(MCGC, India) which has long standing tradition excellence occupied with caring, stimulating and open ethos.
Welcome Edison World School Abu Dhabi. This school has been set collaboration with the 138 year old Mayo College General Council(MCGC, India) which has long standing tradition excellence occupied with caring, stimulating and open ethos.

original text:
Manufacturers added 24,000 workers April, the seventh consecutive month with robust hiring growth the sector, averaging 26,571 per month over that time frame. such, the latest jobs numbers confirm that the labor market has tightened significantly, with manufacturers increasing employment rather strong 19,000 per month average since the end 2016. Since the end the Great Recession, manufacturing employment has risen 1,202,000 workers, with 12,655,000 employees the sector this report. That the highest level manufacturing employment since December 2008. More importantly, healthy jobs growth also leading higher wages. Average weekly earnings for production and nonsupervisory employees the manufacturing sector rose $907.78, 4.2 percent over the past months.
Despite the healthy increase manufacturing employment, nonfarm payrolls increased just 164,000 April. While this represented improvement from the gain 135,000 March, was below the consensus estimate around 190,000. Meanwhile, the unemployment rate dropped 3.9 percent April and continued the lowest level since December 2000. addition, the so-called "real" unemployment rate, which includes discouraged, other "marginally attached" workers, fell from 8.0 percent 7.8 percent, the lowest level since July 2001.
Other measures manufacturing activity were also heartening, with continued growth and solid economic outlook. The ISM Manufacturing Purchasing Managers' Index declined 57.3 April, the lowest level since July 2017, but even with softer growth production, exports and hiring, the latest survey remained consistent with healthy expansions the sector. Along those lines, new orders were robust, with that measure higher threshold suggesting very strong growth for straight months. The sample comments tend echo that finding, with respondents noting healthy growth activity and promising outlook, even they cite some trade worries. the same time, manufacturers report accelerated input costs, with the data raw material prices levels not seen seven years. There were similarly encouraging findings the latest manufacturing survey from the Dallas Federal Reserve Bank and the most recent factory orders data.
Improved international economic growth has also helped, with exports building the early months 2018 the rebound global demand 2017. goods exports totaled $280.56 billion through the first three months 2018, 6.34 percent from the year-to-date total $263.83 billion 2017. addition, the U.S. trade deficit fell back earth March after soaring the highest level since October 2008 the prior release. The trade deficit declined from $57.74 billion February $48.96 billion March six-month low. More importantly, goods exports rose new all-time high, $140.88 billion. With that said, worth noting that the U.S. dollar has risen 3.4 percent since January against major currencies, according the Federal Reserve. Still, the dollar remains 8.6 percent lower than was the end 2016, helping the overall competitiveness U.S. exports.
noted earlier, manufacturers continue report pickup pricing pressures. Indeed, the personal consumption expenditures (PCEs) deflator has risen 2.0 percent over the past months, the fastest year-over-year pace since February 2017. Excluding food and energy, core PCE inflation rose 1.9 percent year-over-year March, accelerating for the seventh consecutive month after bottoming out 1.3 percent August. the conclusion its May 1-2 meeting, the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) acknowledged that inflation had approached the committee's 2-percent goal, but expected, participants chose not raise short-term rates. The Federal Reserve did note continued strength the U.S. economy, especially the labor market, and the FOMC widely expected hike the federal funds rate again its June 12-13 meeting for the second time far this year.
Given the healthy gains manufacturing employment described above, the latest Job Openings and Labor Turnover Survey report will one the highlights this week. February, there were 426,000 job postings the sector, with hiring the highest level since November 2007. Indeed, manufacturers continue cite their inability attract and retain quality workers the primary challenge for their business, according the most recent NAM survey. There will also additional data inflation, with updates consumer and producer prices. Other releases out this week include new figures consumer credit, consumer sentiment and small business optimism.
ADP reported that manufacturers added 10,000 workers April, easing somewhat but extending the 18,000 gain March. Through the first four months 2018, the manufacturing sector has hired additional 17,942 employees per month, from average 14,823 per month for all 2017. The data show strong growth hiring activity since the end 2016, especially production and the overall economic outlook have improved. contrast, manufacturing employment was more sluggish 2016, illustrating the turnaround the labor market since then. More importantly, continued strength job growth expected moving forward.
Meanwhile, total nonfarm private employment increased 204,000 April, off from 228,000 March. Nonetheless, was the fifth consecutive month with job growth least 200,000 the U.S. economy, according ADP's estimates. Over the past four months, nonfarm payrolls have risen 228,542 workers per month, from average 185,415 per month for all 2017. This yet another indication that the U.S. labor market remains quite robust.
April, the largest employment growth included professional and business services (up 58,000), education and health services (up 39,000), leisure and hospitality (up 36,000), construction (up 27,000) and trade, transportation and utilities (up 14,000). Small and medium-sized businesses (i.e., those with fewer than 500 employees) accounted for 73.5 percent the net job growth for the month.
The Bureau Labor Statistics reported that manufacturers added 24,000 workers April, extending the 22,000 gain March. was the seventh consecutive month with robust hiring growth the sector, averaging 26,571 per month over that time frame. such, the latest jobs numbers confirm that the labor market has tightened significantly, with manufacturers increasing employment rather strong 19,000 per month average since the end 2016. That quite turnaround from the sluggish job growth 2016, and sign that firms have continued accelerate their hiring the economic outlook has strengthened and demand and production have improved considerably.
The latest report very encouraging. Strong job growth April shows that economic confidence remains high the wake historic, pro-growth tax reform. Moreover, manufacturers continue invest, raise wages and hire more workers they expand their businesses and plan for the future. Congress and the president delivered their promises, and now manufacturers, through our "Keeping Our Promise" campaign, are showing how are delivering ours.
Along those lines, average weekly earnings for production and nonsupervisory employees the manufacturing sector rose from $901.39 March $907.78 April. That translated into whopping 4.2 percent increase over the past months, from $871.10 April 2017, which further illustrates the strength the labor market right now.
The Census Bureau reported that private manufacturing construction spending slipped for the second straight month, down 1.1 percent March after decreasing 0.9 percent February. The value construction put place the sector rose $67.73 billion the annual rate January, the fastest pace since May 2017, but eased $67.14 billion February and $66.38 billion March. Overall, construction the sector has drifted lower since achieving the all-time high $82.13 billion May 2015. Along those lines, manufacturing construction has declined 6.9 percent year-over-year. Even with some recent softness, construction activity should accelerate the coming months with the solid economic outlook and robust growth demand, production and hiring.
Overall, private nonresidential construction spending declined 0.4 percent March, but with year-over-year growth modest 2.2 percent. the latest figures, the data showed mixed results. There were more dollars spent construction projects the amusement and recreation (up 4.5 percent), religious (up 3.5 percent), communications (up 1.6 percent), office (up 1.1 percent) and educational (up 0.9 percent) segments, among others. addition manufacturing, the other major sectors with decreased construction spending March included health care (down 3.5 percent), commercial (down 2.2 percent) and lodging (down 0.4 percent).
Meanwhile, private residential construction spending fell 3.5 percent March, but with 5.3 percent growth over the past months. the latest data, single-family and multifamily construction declined 0.4 percent and 2.7 percent March, respectively. More encouragingly, single-family activity jumped 9.7 percent year-over-year. addition those components, public construction spending was flat March, with gain 3.0 percent over the past year.
The Dallas Federal Reserve Bank reported that manufacturing activity continued expand solidly April. This trend seen continuing over the next six months well, with 53.7 percent respondents predicting higher costs and just 4.6 percent anticipating declines.

More than half respondents anticipate new orders, production, capacity utilization and shipments increasing over the next six months, with 41.3 percent and 39.1 percent planning hire more workers and spend more capital investments, respectively.

Excluding transportation equipment, new orders for manufactured goods increased 0.3 percent March. Overall, new factory orders have trended sharply higher over the past year. Nonetheless, core capital goods nondefense capital goods excluding aircraft fell 0.4 percent March. These can often seen proxy for capital spending the U.S. economy, and such, the data suggest that capital investments might have been softer March than might prefer. Encouragingly, much like the headline number, there was healthy gain 6.8 percent over the past months.
Durable goods demand increased 2.6 percent March, but excluding transportation equipment, new orders edged just 0.1 percent. the same time, sales nondurable goods increased 0.5 percent March. Looking specifically durable goods activity March, new orders strengthened for primary metals (up 2.1 percent), computers and electronic products (up 1.0 percent), electrical equipment, appliances and components (up 0.6 percent) and fabricated metal products (up 0.3 percent). contrast, sales decreased for machinery (down 1.9 percent), furniture and related products (down 1.5 percent) and motor vehicles and parts (down 1.0 percent).
Meanwhile, shipments manufactured goods rose 0.4 percent March, with durable and nondurable goods shipments increasing 0.4 percent and 0.5 percent, respectively. year-over-year basis, factory shipments have risen 6.8 percent since March 2017, 6.6 percent with transportation equipment excluded. Core capital goods shipments pulled back 0.1 percent from February's all-time high, but with year-over-year growth 7.6 percent.
The Bureau Economic Analysis and the Census Bureau reported that the U.S. trade deficit fell back earth March after soaring the highest level since October 2008 the prior release. The trade deficit declined from $57.74 billion February $48.96 billion March six-month low. More importantly, goods exports rose new all-time high, from $137.13 billion $140.88 billion. The Institute for Supply Management (ISM) reported that manufacturing activity continued expand modestly April, even pulled back for the second straight month from February's best reading since May 2004. the same time, personal income rose 0.3 percent for the second straight month March. Since March 2017, personal incomes have risen 3.6 percent. such, income growth has remained modest levels. other news, the PCE deflator was unchanged March, pulling back from gain 0.2 percent February. The drag the PCE deflator March stemmed largely from energy costs, which decreased 2.8 percent. Core inflation, which excludes food and energy, increased 0.2 percent March.

Durable and nondurable goods labor productivity increased 1.3 percent and 0.6 percent the first quarter, respectively.
The latest release featured revisions the historical data. 2017, manufacturing labor productivity increased 0.4 percent, down from the prior estimate 0.6 percent. Output and unit labor costs rose 1.5 percent and 1.1 percent 2017, respectively. The annual figure for manufacturing labor productivity growth was slightly better than the average from 2013 2017, which registered just 0.2 percent. Indeed, sluggish productivity growth continues one the larger frustrations the U.S. economy the aftermath the Great Recession. comparison, the average growth rate for manufacturing labor productivity registered 3.9 percent both the 1990-2000 and 2002-2008 time frames, the two prior economic recoveries.
Meanwhile, nonfarm labor productivity rose 0.7 percent the first quarter, from the 0.3 percent increase the fourth quarter. Hourly compensation costs jumped 3.4 percent the first quarter, with unit labor costs 2.7 percent. Similar the manufacturing data described above, nonfarm labor productivity has slowed considerably since the recession, averaging 0.7 percent per year from 2011 2017. That remains lower than the 2.7 percent annual growth rate average from 2000 2007.

original text:
DRAGON-STAR and its successor project DRAGON-STAR Plus come end after almost six years operation, having organised its last Think Tank Workshop THE FUTURE MANUFACTURING: 'INDUSTRY 4.0' MEETS 'MADE CHINA 2025' November 2017 Shanghai.
About participants, experts, representatives from universities and industry, policy makers and stakeholders from China and Europe discussed and critically highlighted the pros and cons Made China 2025 and its counterpart Industry 4.0 Europe.
All experts agreed that lot jobs will lost their way but significant new profile jobs will created.
According Mr.Kay Matzner, Fraunhofer Institute for Factory Operation and Automation IFF, major characteristics Industry 4.0 Europe, mainly Germany, are the shift from mass production individual products and that salaries regions will not important any more. Industrial Ecosystems are located close the customer produce customised products and new job profiles will not physically demanding any more due the increasing use robots. This raises already and will increasingly raise ethical questions after Industry 4.0.
next step following SME involvement pick technologies easier and get better access Industry 4.0. Research will also need connected better, standardisation will need improved, and the legal framework Industry 4.0 will need evolved. Also international cooperation will have increase order assure win-win situation between Europe and China!
Prof. Zhang Chidong, CASTED China Academy Science and Technology for Development, highlighted why Made China 2025 was put forward China. According him Chinese companies were still the bottom the global industrial value chain and the goal Made China 2025 was reach the medium and top level global industrial value chain. The support and encouragement SMEs and enterprises play very important role the formation strategic alliances China.
Mr. Roland Sommer, Industry 4.0 Austria, outlined that the digitalisation the complete supply chain goes hand-in-hand with the switch from hardware software, from products services and from single product platforms. Consequently the business model key reassure the positive effect economy!
also highlighted that was about cooperation between industry, science, policy makers, regions, and other stakeholders addressing social issues, i.e. the great fear workers losing their jobs.
Expert groups need work strategies and RDI developing technology road maps bridging the gaps between research needs and science, norms and standards, business models targeted towards SMEs, security and safety issues, qualifications and continuous trainings, etc.
Smart logistics need address the whole supply chain, platforms need exchange views international developments and regional strategies facing the concrete effects Industry 4.0.
Prof. Tang Tang, Tongji University, stated that the mobile communication biggest market opportunity became the world's fastest growing technological innovation. The Internet Things and the Internet Services were important forces leading the world's strategic developments.
Also Cloud Computing, Big data and Artificial Intelligence will guide China three steps, i.e. Made China 2025 manufacturing giant, Made China 2035 manufacturing power and Made China 2045 world power.
Talent cultivation will the main requirement for intelligent manufacturing, i.e. cultivating systematic talents with backgrounds. Also according him international cooperation key talent cultivation.
New technologies will add new aspects the qualifications, cross competences and soft skills will become crucial, such intercultural skills, problem solving skills and qualifications, such handling complex systems, taking responsibility for whole processes production lines. parallel social issues need addressed and systematically communicated and employees' fears losing their jobs need discussed and taken seriously.
Future challenges will similar China and Europe. SME will have incorporate the new technologies! Open policy strategies both sides will bring lot opportunities.
Policy makers both sides need become aware that there big opportunity and importance collaborate intelligent manufacturing, besides the different culture, different industries, different policies, and different market. Goal win-win situation for Europe and China and trustful environment allowing the learning from each other both sides can reach their targets. 'Only through cooperation can further together' (Roland Sommer, Industry 4.0 Austria).
After its last event THE FUTURE MANUFACTURING: 'INDUSTRY 4.0' MEETS 'MADE CHINA 2025' November 2017 Shanghai, DRAGON-STAR Plus and ENRICH-China share booth China Hi-Tech Fair 2017 between 16-21 November 2017 Shenzhen.
The China Hi-Tech Fair international trade fair for electronics and electrical engineering, which takes place annually the Convention Exhibition Centre Shenzhen. The fair the largest Hi-Tech fair China and therefore the largest Chinese technology fair. with exhibitors from about countries the most extensive public platform exchange latest technologies, concepts and solutions electronic and electrical engineering industry. also focuses advanced technologies and products the fields energy saving, environmental protection, production high-end appliances, new energies and materials.
With this last important promotion campaign and presence the largest Chinese technology fair, DRAGON-STAR and DRAGON-STAR Plus close the doors after having successfully supported the European and Chinese research communities for almost six years establish collaborations under Horizon 2020 and beyond and after fruitfully establishing platform for policy makers enhancing Sino-European STI cooperation.

The support and encouragement SMEs and enterprises play very important role the formation strategic alliances China.
Mr. Roland Sommer, Industry 4.0 Austria, outlined that the digitalisation the complete supply chain goes hand-in-hand with the switch from hardware software, from products services and from single product platforms. Consequently the business model key reassure the positive effect economy!
also highlighted that was about cooperation between industry, science, policy makers, regions, and other stakeholders addressing social issues, i.e. the great fear workers losing their jobs.
Expert groups need work strategies and RDI developing technology road maps bridging the gaps between research needs and science, norms and standards, business models targeted towards SMEs, security and safety issues, qualifications and continuous trainings, etc.
Smart logistics need address the whole supply chain, platforms need exchange views international developments and regional strategies facing the concrete effects Industry 4.0.
 Tang Tang, Tongji University, stated that the mobile communication biggest market opportunity became the world's fastest growing technological innovation. The Internet Things and the Internet Services were important forces leading the world's strategic developments.
Also Cloud Computing, Big data and Artificial Intelligence will guide China three steps, i.e. Made China 2025 manufacturing giant, Made China 2035 manufacturing power and Made China 2045 world power.
Talent cultivation will the main requirement for intelligent manufacturing, i.e. cultivating systematic talents with backgrounds. Also according him international cooperation key talent cultivation.
New technologies will add new aspects the qualifications, cross competences and soft skills will become crucial, such intercultural skills, problem solving skills and qualifications, such handling complex systems, taking responsibility for whole processes production lines. parallel social issues need addressed and systematically communicated and employees' fears losing their jobs need discussed and taken seriously.
Future challenges will similar China and Europe. SME will have incorporate the new technologies! Open policy strategies both sides will bring lot opportunities.
Policy makers both sides need become aware that there big opportunity and importance collaborate intelligent manufacturing, besides the different culture, different industries, different policies, and different market. Goal win-win situation for Europe and China and trustful environment allowing the learning from each other both sides can reach their targets. 'Only through cooperation can further together' (Roland Sommer, Industry 4.0 Austria).
After its last event THE FUTURE MANUFACTURING: 'INDUSTRY 4.0' MEETS 'MADE CHINA 2025' November 2017 Shanghai, DRAGON-STAR Plus and ENRICH-China share booth China Hi-Tech Fair 2017 between 16-21 November 2017 Shenzhen.
The China Hi-Tech Fair international trade fair for electronics and electrical engineering, which takes place annually the Convention Exhibition Centre Shenzhen. The fair the largest Hi-Tech fair China and therefore the largest Chinese technology fair. with exhibitors from about countries the most extensive public platform exchange latest technologies, concepts and solutions electronic and electrical engineering industry. also focuses advanced technologies and products the fields energy saving, environmental protection, production high-end appliances, new energies and materials.
With this last important promotion campaign and presence the largest Chinese technology fair, DRAGON-STAR and DRAGON-STAR Plus close the doors after having successfully supported the European and Chinese research communities for almost six years establish collaborations under Horizon 2020 and beyond and after fruitfully establishing platform for policy makers enhancing Sino-European STI cooperation.
